comments.body: Oof, [the Dockerfile build](https://quay.io/repository/skopeo/ci/build/7d2d1016-77fd-438e-948d-f8e11281c5d9) is currently broken as well, gunna needa fix that too...

...okay, local testing shows the Dockerfile now builds successfully.  I added a notification to the auto-builder (on quay.io) for this image: It should now post future build-failure notices to our podman-monitor list.

> Oof, the Dockerfile build is currently broken as well, gunna needa fix that too...

To be explicit, those are the `GO111MODULE` changes, which are otherwise unrelated?

---

The `cross` task is failing for what seems to be a Brew server failure, I have triggered a re-run.
@mtrmac PTAL, I fixed the whitespace and bumped up the timeout a bit to see if that helps the cross task.

@cevich Looking at https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/issues/11119 or https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/pull/11366 , the `brew` failure seems not to be a temporary situation (although https://status.bintray.com does currently report a scheduled maintenance).

Is the `brew` version in the macOS environment new enough, or could it be updated? If not, a `brew update` before package installation might well pay off in time.

> But even if it gets CI unblocked for a week and then we can revert

I'm good either way.  The same key-name can be added to individual tasks for more specific/specialized timeout values, I only stuck in a global one out of sheer laziness.
I have filed a completely uninformed #1329 with changes from this PR + `brew update`, just to see what happens.

> To be explicit, those are the GO111MODULE changes, which are otherwise unrelated?

Yes, mostly unrelated.  Though auto-builder on quay was specifically targeting the master branch.  When I fixed that, I noticed the image build has been failing for months, so, yeah, :disappointed: Anyway, I also added some monitoring, so we'll now get e-mails to the podman-monitor list any time it breaks in the future.
